articles.headline: Under-Construction Power Plant Collapse in Tamil Nadu Claims Nine Lives
articles.explainer:
• An arch collapse at an under-construction thermal power plant in Tamil Nadu resulted in the death of at least nine workers and left one critically injured.
• The incident occurred during the structural work of a boiler unit, raising concerns about construction safety protocols and regulatory oversight.
• Preliminary investigations point towards potential lapses in structural design or material quality, alongside inadequate safety measures for workers.
• This tragic event highlights the critical need for stricter enforcement of industrial safety standards and regular inspections in high-risk infrastructure projects.
• The state government has announced an ex-gratia payment for the victims' families and initiated a high-level inquiry into the causes of the collapse.
gsSubTopics: Disaster Management
Infrastructure
Industrial Policy
Role of regulatory bodies
prelimsPoints: Location of the incident (Tamil Nadu)
Type of plant (under-construction thermal power plant)
Number of casualties
Regulatory bodies involved in industrial safety (e.g., Directorate General of Factory Advice Service & Labour Institutes - DGFASLI)
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the challenges in ensuring industrial safety in India's rapidly expanding infrastructure sector.
Discuss the role of government, private contractors, and labor unions in preventing such accidents.
Examine the effectiveness of existing disaster management protocols and compensation mechanisms for industrial accidents.
Suggest measures for strengthening regulatory frameworks and enforcement to prevent future mishaps.
articles.imageDescription: Rescue operations underway at the collapsed power plant site in Tamil Nadu.
articles.trivia: India is the third-largest producer and second-largest consumer of electricity globally, with thermal power accounting for a significant share of its generation capacity.

articles.headline: Veteran Politician Vijay Kumar Malhotra Passes Away at 93
articles.explainer:
• Veteran Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) leader and former Member of Parliament, Vijay Kumar Malhotra, passed away at the age of 93.
• Malhotra had a long and distinguished political career, serving as a Member of Parliament from South Delhi constituency for multiple terms (1989–2008).
• He also served as a Member of the Delhi Legislative Assembly (2008–2013) and held various significant positions within the BJP.
• Known for his oratorical skills and deep understanding of parliamentary procedures, he was a prominent figure in Delhi and national politics.
• His demise marks the end of an era for a politician who witnessed and shaped significant political developments in post-independence India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution - historical underpinnings
Parliament and State Legislatures - structure, functioning
Prominent personalities
prelimsPoints: Name: Vijay Kumar Malhotra
Age: 93
Political party: BJP
Key roles: MP (South Delhi), MLA (Delhi)
Period of service as MP and MLA
articles.imageDescription: File photo of Vijay Kumar Malhotra addressing a public gathering.
articles.trivia: Vijay Kumar Malhotra was also a noted academician, having served as a professor before entering full-time politics.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Electoral Bond Scheme with Enhanced Transparency Directives
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, has largely upheld the constitutional validity of the Electoral Bond Scheme, while introducing new directives for greater transparency.
• The court mandated that all political parties must disclose the details of electoral bonds received, including donor information, to the Election Commission of India (ECI) within a specified timeframe.
• This ruling aims to balance the need for anonymous political funding, cited by the government, with the public's right to information regarding financial contributions to political parties.
• Critics argue that the scheme, even with new directives, still lacks complete transparency as donor identities are not publicly revealed by the ECI.
• The judgment will likely reshape the landscape of political funding in India, prompting debates on democratic accountability and funding reforms.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution - significant provisions
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Separation of powers
Electoral reforms
Transparency and accountability
prelimsPoints: Electoral Bond Scheme: introduced in 2018
Purpose: to cleanse political funding
Key features: bearer instrument, anonymous to public, purchased from SBI
Supreme Court's stance: upheld with transparency directives
Role of ECI in collecting donor details
mainsPoints: Analyze the Supreme Court's verdict on electoral bonds in light of democratic principles and the right to information.
Discuss the pros and cons of electoral bonds as a mechanism for political funding in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the new transparency directives in curbing illicit funding and promoting accountability.
Suggest further electoral reforms needed to ensure fair and transparent elections.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building facade.
articles.trivia: The State Bank of India (SBI) is the only authorized bank to issue and encash electoral bonds.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Persistent Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the fifth consecutive meeting.
• The decision comes as the central bank continues to prioritize inflation control, despite signs of moderating economic growth.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted global uncertainties and volatile food prices as key risks to the inflation outlook.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Experts anticipate the RBI to maintain a cautious stance, with rate cuts unlikely until inflation shows a sustained downward trend.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Current Repo Rate: 6.5% (unchanged)
Number of consecutive meetings unchanged: 5th
Primary objective of RBI's monetary policy: Price stability (inflation control)
Tools used by RBI: Repo rate, reverse repo rate, CRR, SLR, OMO
Composition of MPC: 6 members (3 RBI, 3 GoI nominees)
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflation in India.
Examine the implications of a prolonged high-interest rate regime on various sectors of the Indian economy.
Evaluate the role of the Monetary Policy Committee in maintaining financial stability and fostering sustainable growth.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The MPC was constituted in India in 2016 to bring transparency and accountability in deciding monetary policy.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Strengthens Indo-Pacific Cooperation and Economic Ties
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming both nations' commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions focused on enhancing cooperation in defense, security, economic partnership, critical and emerging technologies, and regional connectivity projects.
• Both sides emphasized the importance of upholding international law and resolving disputes peacefully, particularly in the context of rising geopolitical tensions.
• Key outcomes included new initiatives for joint defense exercises, collaboration in clean energy, and boosting supply chain resilience.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of strategic interests between India and Japan, vital for regional stability and prosperity.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Format: Annual Strategic Dialogue
Key areas of cooperation: Defense, Economy, Tech, Connectivity
Shared vision: Free, Open, Inclusive Indo-Pacific
Other related groupings: QUAD (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue)
mainsPoints: Assess the significance of the India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the various pillars of cooperation between India and Japan, highlighting their contribution to regional security and economic development.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in deepening India-Japan defense and economic ties amidst evolving global dynamics.
How does India's 'Act East Policy' align with its strategic partnership with Japan?
articles.imageDescription: Ministers from India and Japan shaking hands during the strategic dialogue.
articles.trivia: Japan is one of the largest investors in India's infrastructure sector, notably the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail project.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Weather Satellite INSAT-3DRS
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched the advanced geostationary weather satellite, INSAT-3DRS, from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
• Carried by the GSLV-F15 rocket, the satellite is designed to provide enhanced meteorological observations and disaster warning services for India and the Indian Ocean region.
• INSAT-3DRS features advanced imaging systems and atmospheric sounders, significantly improving weather forecasting capabilities and cyclone tracking.
• This mission reinforces India's self-reliance in space technology and its commitment to contributing to global weather and climate monitoring efforts.
• The data from INSAT-3DRS will be crucial for agriculture, disaster management, and various climate research applications.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
Disaster Management
prelimsPoints: Satellite name: INSAT-3DRS
Launch vehicle: GSLV-F15
Purpose: Advanced meteorological observations, disaster warning
Orbit type: Geostationary
Launch site: Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's space program in contributing to national development and disaster management.
Evaluate the role of advanced weather satellites like INSAT-3DRS in improving climate resilience and agricultural productivity.
Examine how ISRO's indigenous capabilities in space technology contribute to India's strategic autonomy.
What are the future prospects for India's space program, particularly in Earth observation and planetary exploration?
articles.imageDescription: A GSLV rocket launching into space from a launchpad.
articles.trivia: India's first dedicated meteorological satellite, Kalpana-1 (formerly MetSat-1), was launched in 2002.

articles.headline: NGT Imposes Hefty Fines on Industries for Environmental Violations in Ganga Basin
articles.explainer:
• The National Green Tribunal (NGT) has imposed significant penalties on several industries located along the Ganga basin for continuous discharge of untreated effluents.
• The NGT's ruling emphasized the 'Polluter Pays' principle and directed immediate measures for compliance with environmental norms and installation of effluent treatment plants.
• This action comes amidst ongoing efforts to rejuvenate the Ganga River and curb industrial pollution, a major challenge for the Namami Gange programme.
• The tribunal also highlighted the failure of local authorities in effective monitoring and enforcement of environmental regulations.
• The judgment underscores the NGT's proactive role in environmental protection and holding polluters accountable for ecological damage.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: National Green Tribunal (NGT): established in 2010, specialized body for environmental justice
Principle applied: Polluter Pays Principle
Affected region: Ganga basin
Related government program: Namami Gange Programme
NGT's powers: can impose fines, issue directions
mainsPoints: Evaluate the effectiveness of the National Green Tribunal (NGT) in upholding environmental justice and promoting sustainable development in India.
Discuss the major challenges in controlling industrial pollution, particularly in river basins like the Ganga, and suggest remedial measures.
Analyze the 'Polluter Pays' principle and its application in Indian environmental law. What are its limitations?
Critically examine the progress and challenges of the Namami Gange programme.
articles.imageDescription: A polluted stretch of a river with industrial discharge in the background.
articles.trivia: The NGT has been instrumental in several landmark judgments, including banning 10-year-old diesel and 15-year-old petrol vehicles in Delhi-NCR.

articles.headline: One Nation, One Ration Card Scheme Enters Phase 3, Covering All Migrant Workers
articles.explainer:
• The 'One Nation, One Ration Card' (ONORC) scheme has successfully entered its third phase, achieving nationwide coverage to include all eligible migrant workers.
• This phase focuses on enhancing technology integration and last-mile delivery to ensure seamless access to subsidized food grains for interstate migrant beneficiaries.
• The scheme, implemented under the National Food Security Act (NFSA), allows beneficiaries to lift their entitled food grains from any Fair Price Shop (FPS) across the country.
• ONORC is a significant step towards ensuring food security and portability of benefits for India's large migrant population, reducing dependency on local ration cards.
• The government plans to further integrate other welfare schemes with ONORC to create a comprehensive safety net for vulnerable sections.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
prelimsPoints: Scheme: One Nation, One Ration Card (ONORC)
Objective: Portability of food security benefits for migrants
Legal basis: National Food Security Act (NFSA) 2013
Mechanism: Aadhaar seeding, ePOS devices at FPS
Current status: Nationwide coverage in Phase 3
mainsPoints: Evaluate the success of the 'One Nation, One Ration Card' scheme in addressing the food security challenges faced by migrant workers in India.
Discuss the technological and logistical hurdles in implementing such a large-scale portability scheme and how they have been overcome.
Analyze the socio-economic impact of ONORC on vulnerable sections of society, particularly during crises like pandemics.
Suggest further reforms needed in the Public Distribution System (PDS) to ensure more efficient and equitable delivery of food grains.
articles.imageDescription: A person receiving ration at a Fair Price Shop using a digital biometric system.
articles.trivia: The ONORC scheme was launched in August 2019, initially in four states, and gradually expanded.

articles.headline: India's Q2 GDP Growth Projected at 7.5%, Driven by Services and Investment
articles.explainer:
• Leading economic agencies and the government's finance ministry have projected India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth for Q2 FY2025 (July-September) at 7.5%.
• This robust growth is primarily attributed to strong performance in the services sector, resilient domestic demand, and a noticeable uptick in private capital expenditure.
• Manufacturing and construction sectors also showed positive momentum, signaling a broad-based recovery and expansion across key economic drivers.
• While global headwinds persist, India's domestic consumption and public investment continue to act as significant buffers, sustaining economic buoyancy.
• The projection reinforces India's position as one of the fastest-growing major economies globally, albeit with ongoing concerns about inflation and fiscal consolidation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Inclusive Growth
prelimsPoints: Q2 FY2025 period: July-September
Projected GDP growth rate: 7.5%
Key drivers: Services, private investment, domestic demand
Economic challenges: Inflation, global headwinds
India's global ranking: Fastest-growing major economy
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's sustained economic growth despite global uncertainties.
Discuss the role of different sectors (agriculture, industry, services) in India's GDP growth and their interlinkages.
Examine the challenges that India needs to address to achieve inclusive and sustainable economic growth.
Evaluate the government's fiscal and monetary policies in fostering economic stability and growth.
articles.imageDescription: A graphical representation of India's economic growth trend.
articles.trivia: India's economy is projected to become the third-largest in the world by 2027.

articles.headline: India Hosts BIMSTEC Foreign Ministers' Meeting, Focus on Regional Connectivity and Trade
articles.explainer:
• India hosted the annual Foreign Ministers' Meeting of the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC) in New Delhi.
• The meeting focused on accelerating regional integration, enhancing connectivity across various modes, and boosting intra-BIMSTEC trade and investment.
• Discussions included progress on the BIMSTEC Master Plan for Transport Connectivity and strategies to tackle climate change, disaster management, and health security in the region.
• India reiterated its commitment to strengthening BIMSTEC as a vital platform for regional cooperation, especially in the context of its 'Neighbourhood First' and 'Act East' policies.
• The ministers emphasized shared challenges and opportunities, aiming to build a more prosperous and resilient Bay of Bengal region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: BIMSTEC: Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation
Member countries: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, Thailand (7 countries)
Host country: India (2025 meeting)
Key focus areas: Connectivity, Trade, Disaster Management, Climate Change
Significance for India: Bridges South Asia and Southeast Asia
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of BIMSTEC for India's foreign policy, particularly in the context of its 'Neighbourhood First' and 'Act East' policies.
Discuss the potential of BIMSTEC to foster regional connectivity, trade, and economic growth in the Bay of Bengal region.
Examine the challenges hindering deeper cooperation within BIMSTEC and suggest measures to overcome them.
Compare and contrast BIMSTEC with SAARC as a regional grouping for India.
articles.imageDescription: A logo representing the BIMSTEC organization with flags of member countries.
articles.trivia: BIMSTEC was established in 1997 through the Bangkok Declaration.

articles.headline: BRO Completes Strategic Shinku La Tunnel, Enhancing All-Weather Connectivity to Ladakh
articles.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) has announced the completion of the strategic Shinku La Tunnel, providing all-weather connectivity to the Zanskar Valley in Ladakh.
• This high-altitude tunnel, located at over 16,000 feet, is a critical infrastructure project for national security and regional development.
• It significantly reduces travel time and improves accessibility to remote border areas, crucial for both military logistics and local communities.
• The tunnel will bypass the challenging Shinku La pass, which remains snow-bound for much of the year, ensuring year-round access.
• This engineering marvel is part of India's broader strategy to enhance border infrastructure and integrate remote regions into the national mainstream.
gsSubTopics: Border areas and their management
Security challenges and their management in border areas
Infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Tunnel name: Shinku La Tunnel
Location: Zanskar Valley, Ladakh (connecting Himachal Pradesh to Ladakh)
Executing agency: Border Roads Organisation (BRO)
Significance: All-weather connectivity, strategic importance
Altitude: Over 16,000 feet
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of developing all-weather connectivity and border infrastructure in regions like Ladakh for India's national security.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) in constructing infrastructure in high-altitude and difficult terrains.
Evaluate the impact of such infrastructure projects on the socio-economic development of border communities.
What role does infrastructure play in integrating remote regions into the national mainstream and ensuring territorial integrity?
articles.imageDescription: An entrance to a newly constructed tunnel in a mountainous region.
articles.trivia: The BRO is responsible for constructing and maintaining road networks in India's border areas and friendly neighboring countries.

articles.headline: Report Highlights Progress and Challenges in National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive report released by the Ministry of Education details the progress made in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across states.
• The report highlights significant strides in curriculum reforms, teacher training, and integration of technology in education at various levels.
• Challenges persist in achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy, equitable access to quality education in remote areas, and adequate funding.
• The NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century, focusing on holistic development, critical thinking, and vocational skills.
• The government reaffirms its commitment to overcoming the remaining hurdles through collaborative efforts with state governments and educational institutions.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020: Replaced NEP 1986
Key pillars: Access, Equity, Quality, Affordability, Accountability
Focus areas: Early Childhood Care and Education (ECCE), FLN, vocational education, digital education
Implementation challenges: Funding, infrastructure, teacher capacity building
Goal: Transform India into a global knowledge superpower
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 in addressing the challenges of India's education system.
Discuss the key initiatives taken under NEP 2020 and their impact on various levels of education.
Examine the major hurdles in the effective implementation of NEP 2020 and suggest strategies to overcome them.
How can public-private partnerships contribute to achieving the goals set by the National Education Policy?
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, illustrating new educational methods.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 recommends increasing public investment in education to 6% of GDP.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy Framework for Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Power has released a comprehensive new policy framework aimed at accelerating the deployment of Electric Vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure across India.
• The policy focuses on streamlining licensing and regulatory processes, offering incentives for private investment, and standardizing charging technologies.
• Key provisions include mandatory charging stations in new commercial and residential buildings, land availability for public charging, and time-bound installation targets.
• This move is critical for boosting EV adoption, reducing carbon emissions, and achieving India's ambitious climate change goals.
• The framework seeks to create a robust and accessible charging ecosystem, addressing one of the primary barriers to EV proliferation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Policy focus: EV charging infrastructure deployment
Ministry: Ministry of Power
Key features: Licensing ease, incentives, standardization, mandatory installations
Objective: Boost EV adoption, reduce emissions
Related scheme: FAME India Scheme (Faster Adoption and Manufacturing of Electric Vehicles)
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges and opportunities in promoting electric vehicle adoption in India.
Analyze how a robust EV charging infrastructure policy can contribute to India's climate change mitigation goals.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private sector investment in building a sustainable EV ecosystem.
What are the socio-economic implications of transitioning to electric mobility for India?
articles.imageDescription: An electric car charging at a public charging station.
articles.trivia: India aims for 30% of its new vehicle sales to be electric by 2030.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Stupa in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has made a significant discovery, unearthing a well-preserved ancient Buddhist stupa in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• The stupa, believed to date back to the 7th-8th century CE, provides valuable insights into the spread of Buddhism in eastern India during that period.
• Excavations have also revealed intricate sculptures, pottery, and inscriptions, shedding light on the architectural and artistic styles of the time.
• This discovery adds to Odisha's rich Buddhist heritage, which includes prominent sites like Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, and Lalitgiri (the 'Diamond Triangle').
• The site is expected to become a new focal point for historical research and cultural tourism, highlighting India's diverse archaeological wealth.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
History of India and Indian National Movement
prelimsPoints: Discovery: Ancient Buddhist stupa
Location: Jajpur district, Odisha
Period: 7th-8th century CE
Discovering agency: Archaeological Survey of India (ASI)
Related sites in Odisha: Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, Lalitgiri
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in understanding India's ancient history and cultural evolution.
Examine the influence of Buddhism on the art, architecture, and social structure of early medieval India, with special reference to Eastern India.
Evaluate the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in preserving and promoting India's cultural heritage.
How can such discoveries boost cultural tourism and local economies?
articles.imageDescription: Excavated ruins of an ancient Buddhist stupa with intricate carvings.
articles.trivia: The 'Diamond Triangle' of Odisha (Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, Lalitgiri) is known for its extensive Buddhist monastic complexes.

articles.headline: Committee Recommends Sweeping Reforms in India's Prison Management System
articles.explainer:
• A high-level committee constituted by the Ministry of Home Affairs has submitted its report recommending comprehensive reforms in India's prison management system.
• The recommendations address issues such as overcrowding, undertrial prisoner rights, staff training, use of technology, and rehabilitation programs.
• The report emphasizes the need for a human rights-based approach to incarceration and suggests amendments to the Prisons Act, 1894, to align with modern correctional philosophies.
• This initiative aims to transform prisons from mere detention centers into correctional and rehabilitative institutions, in line with global best practices.
• Implementation of these reforms is expected to significantly improve the conditions of prisoners and the overall efficiency of the criminal justice system.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
prelimsPoints: Committee: High-level committee by MHA
Focus: Prison management reforms
Key issues: Overcrowding, undertrial rights, staff training, technology, rehabilitation
Legislation to be updated: Prisons Act, 1894
Goal: Correctional and rehabilitative institutions
mainsPoints: Discuss the major challenges faced by India's prison system and how they impact human rights and the criminal justice system.
Analyze the recommendations of the high-level committee on prison reforms. How can these be effectively implemented?
Examine the concept of 'correctional administration' and its relevance in modern penology. What steps are needed to transition Indian prisons towards this model?
What is the role of technology and community participation in improving prison management and rehabilitation outcomes?
articles.imageDescription: A general view of a prison facility from above, highlighting the need for reform.
articles.trivia: India has one of the highest proportions of undertrial prisoners globally, with over two-thirds of inmates awaiting trial.

articles.headline: PLI Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell Battery Manufacturing Gains Momentum
articles.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing is gaining significant traction, attracting major domestic and international players.
• The scheme aims to boost indigenous manufacturing capabilities for high-efficiency batteries, crucial for electric vehicles and renewable energy storage.
• Government data indicates substantial investment commitments and progress in setting up gigafactories under the scheme.
• This initiative is vital for reducing India's reliance on battery imports, enhancing energy security, and creating a robust domestic supply chain for green technologies.
• The success of the ACC PLI scheme is expected to propel India towards becoming a global hub for advanced battery production and innovation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth, development and employment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Industrial Policy
prelimsPoints: Scheme: PLI for ACC Battery Manufacturing
Objective: Boost indigenous manufacturing, reduce imports
Applications: EVs, renewable energy storage
Key term: Gigafactory (large-scale battery production plant)
Government initiative: Part of Atmanirbhar Bharat
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing for India's economic growth and energy security.
Analyze how the PLI scheme promotes 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in critical technology sectors like EV batteries.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in establishing a robust domestic supply chain for advanced batteries in India.
What role do such incentive schemes play in attracting foreign direct investment and fostering technological innovation?
articles.imageDescription: A graphic illustrating the components of an advanced chemistry cell battery.
articles.trivia: Lithium-ion batteries are currently the most common type of ACC battery used in EVs.

articles.headline: UN General Assembly Debates Climate Financing Ahead of COP30
articles.explainer:
• The United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) held high-level discussions on climate financing, emphasizing the urgent need for developed nations to fulfill their commitments.
• Member states debated mechanisms to mobilize greater financial resources for climate change adaptation and mitigation in developing countries, ahead of COP30.
• Developing nations, including India, reiterated calls for the operationalization of the Loss and Damage Fund and for developed countries to meet the $100 billion annual climate finance goal.
• The discussions highlighted the growing divergence between developed and developing nations on historical responsibility and current financial obligations.
• The outcome of these debates will be crucial in shaping the agenda and negotiations for the upcoming 30th Conference of the Parties (COP30).
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Forum: UN General Assembly (UNGA)
Topic: Climate Financing
Upcoming conference: COP30
Key demands from developing nations: $100 billion goal, Loss and Damage Fund operationalization
Principle: Common but Differentiated Responsibilities (CBDR)
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges and inequities in global climate financing mechanisms, particularly from the perspective of developing countries like India.
Discuss the significance of the Loss and Damage Fund and the $100 billion climate finance goal in achieving global climate justice.
Evaluate India's stance on climate change negotiations and its efforts towards both mitigation and adaptation.
How do geopolitical dynamics influence international cooperation on climate change and resource mobilization?
articles.imageDescription: The United Nations General Assembly hall during a session on climate change.
articles.trivia: The $100 billion annual climate finance goal was first pledged by developed countries at COP15 in Copenhagen in 2009.

articles.headline: India Announces Ambitious New Policy to Achieve 500 GW Renewable Energy Target by 2030
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy unveiled a new comprehensive policy framework to accelerate India's journey towards its ambitious target of 500 GW renewable energy capacity by 2030.
• The policy introduces enhanced incentives for solar and wind energy projects, streamlines land acquisition processes, and promotes green hydrogen production.
• It also focuses on strengthening the national grid infrastructure to integrate a higher share of intermittent renewable energy sources.
• This aggressive push aims to significantly reduce India's carbon footprint, enhance energy security, and create new economic opportunities in the green sector.
• The policy is expected to attract substantial domestic and foreign investment, solidifying India's leadership in global renewable energy transition.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Target: 500 GW renewable energy capacity
Deadline: 2030
Ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
Key focus: Solar, wind, green hydrogen, grid integration
Impact: Reduce carbon footprint, enhance energy security
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India's 500 GW renewable energy target by 2030 for its energy security and climate change commitments.
Discuss the key policy measures introduced to achieve this ambitious target and the challenges in their implementation.
Evaluate the role of green hydrogen in India's renewable energy transition and its potential for economic growth.
How can India ensure grid stability and efficient integration of high shares of intermittent renewable energy sources?
articles.imageDescription: A vast solar power farm stretching across a landscape under a clear sky.
articles.trivia: India's renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) is currently the fourth largest in the world.

articles.headline: Global Digital Public Infrastructure Summit Concludes with Focus on Inclusive Tech Adoption
articles.explainer:
• India successfully hosted the Global Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) Summit, bringing together leaders and experts from over 50 countries.
• The summit focused on leveraging DPIs like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker to foster inclusive growth, financial inclusion, and efficient public service delivery worldwide.
• Discussions centered on developing common frameworks for interoperable digital identity, payment, and data exchange systems.
• India showcased its 'India Stack' as a model for other developing nations to build their own DPIs, emphasizing local adaptation and open-source principles.
• The summit concluded with a joint declaration on promoting equitable access to digital technologies and bridging the global digital divide.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
IT and Computers
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Summit: Global Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) Summit
Host: India
Key Indian DPIs: Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker (part of India Stack)
Objective: Inclusive growth, financial inclusion, public service delivery
Concept: Open-source, interoperable digital systems
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its transformative potential for governance and socio-economic development, with special reference to India's experience.
Analyze how the 'India Stack' has contributed to financial inclusion and efficient public service delivery in the country.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for developing nations in adopting and adapting DPI models for their specific contexts.
What role can global cooperation play in bridging the digital divide and ensuring equitable access to digital technologies?
articles.imageDescription: A graphic illustrating various components of digital public infrastructure such as identity, payments, and data exchange.
articles.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in August 2023, making it one of the world's most successful real-time payment systems.

highlights: Under-construction power plant collapse in Tamil Nadu kills nine, raising concerns about industrial safety and regulatory oversight.
Supreme Court largely upholds Electoral Bond Scheme but mandates enhanced transparency with donor details to ECI, sparking debates on political funding.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5% for fifth consecutive meeting, prioritizing inflation control amidst global uncertainties.
India and Japan strengthen strategic partnership, focusing on Indo-Pacific cooperation, defense, and economic ties.
ISRO successfully launches advanced weather satellite INSAT-3DRS, boosting India's meteorological and disaster warning capabilities.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'One Nation, One Ration Card' (ONORC) scheme, consider the following statements:
1. It allows beneficiaries to lift their entitled food grains from any Fair Price Shop across the country.
2. The scheme is implemented under the Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana.
3. Aadhaar seeding is a crucial component for the portability of benefits under ONORC.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The core objective of ONORC is to provide portability of food security benefits. Statement 2 is incorrect. The scheme is implemented under the National Food Security Act (NFSA), not PMGKAY. PMGKAY was a temporary scheme during the pandemic. Statement 3 is correct. Aadhaar seeding is essential for biometric authentication and ensuring that benefits reach the genuine beneficiaries across states.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements regarding the National Green Tribunal (NGT) are correct?
1. It was established in 2010 under the National Green Tribunal Act.
2. It is mandated to apply the 'Polluter Pays' principle and the 'Precautionary Principle'.
3. Its jurisdiction covers all civil cases relating to environmental protection and conservation of forests and other natural resources.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The NGT was established in 2010 to provide for effective and expeditious disposal of cases relating to environmental protection. It is guided by the principles of natural justice, including the 'Polluter Pays' principle and the 'Precautionary Principle'. Its jurisdiction indeed covers all civil cases relating to environmental protection.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following pairs:

1. INSAT-3DRS: Weather Satellite
2. GSLV-F15: Heavy-lift Launch Vehicle
3. Shinku La Tunnel: All-weather connectivity to Sikkim

Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Pair 1 is correctly matched. INSAT-3DRS is an advanced geostationary weather satellite. Pair 2 is incorrectly matched. While GSLV is a launch vehicle, GSLV-F15 is specifically mentioned as the launch vehicle for INSAT-3DRS and is a medium-lift launch vehicle, not typically categorized as 'heavy-lift' compared to, say, Falcon Heavy or upcoming ISRO heavy-lift variants. Pair 3 is incorrectly matched. The Shinku La Tunnel provides all-weather connectivity to the Zanskar Valley in Ladakh, not Sikkim.

prelimsMocks.question:
Regarding the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Manufacturing, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. The scheme aims to boost indigenous manufacturing of high-efficiency batteries.
2. It is primarily targeted at reducing India's reliance on battery imports for electric vehicles.
3. The scheme falls under the purview of the Ministry of Heavy Industries.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. The PLI scheme for ACC batteries aims to boost indigenous manufacturing and reduce import dependence for critical components like EV batteries. Statement 3 is also correct. The PLI scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage is implemented by the Ministry of Heavy Industries.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a core component of India's 'India Stack' Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI)?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Aadhaar (digital identity), UPI (payments), and DigiLocker (data exchange/document management) are core components of the India Stack, providing foundational digital public infrastructure. The National Optical Fibre Network (now BharatNet) is an infrastructure project providing broadband connectivity, which supports DPIs, but is not considered a *core component* of the 'India Stack' in the same conceptual sense as the identity, payments, and data layers.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the Supreme Court's verdict on the Electoral Bond Scheme. Discuss how the recent directives for enhanced transparency aim to balance anonymous political funding with democratic accountability in India.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Governance, Transparency & Accountability, Electoral Reforms
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Electoral Bond Scheme and its stated objectives. Briefly explain the Supreme Court's previous and current stance. Discuss the arguments for and against the scheme, focusing on anonymity vs. transparency. Elaborate on the new directives for disclosure to the ECI and evaluate their effectiveness in ensuring accountability. Conclude with the implications for political funding and electoral reforms in India.

mainsMocks.question: India's ambitious target of achieving 500 GW renewable energy capacity by 2030 requires robust policy support and infrastructural development. Discuss the key policy measures being undertaken and the challenges in integrating a high share of intermittent renewable energy sources into the national grid.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's renewable energy targets and its significance for energy security and climate goals. Detail the new policy measures (incentives, land acquisition, green hydrogen, grid strengthening). Discuss challenges such as intermittency, grid stability, storage solutions, land availability, and financing. Conclude with potential solutions and the overall impact on India's energy transition.

mainsMocks.question: The 'One Nation, One Ration Card' (ONORC) scheme is a significant step towards ensuring food security for migrant workers. Evaluate its success and discuss the technological and logistical challenges that have been addressed or still persist in its nationwide implementation.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government Policies, Welfare Schemes, Social Justice
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by defining ONORC and its objective for migrant workers under NFSA. Discuss its success in providing portability and enhancing food security. Elaborate on technological aspects like Aadhaar seeding, ePOS, and data integration. Address logistical challenges such as awareness, connectivity in remote areas, and ensuring last-mile delivery. Conclude with its overall impact and future prospects for comprehensive welfare.

quickBytes.text: The National Food Security Act (NFSA) 2013 aims to provide subsidized food grains to approximately two-thirds of India's population.
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.
The 'Polluter Pays Principle' states that those who produce pollution should bear the costs of managing it to prevent damage to human health or the environment.
The Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC) comprises seven member states.
India's 'Act East Policy' is a diplomatic initiative to promote economic, strategic, and cultural relations with the Indo-Pacific region.
The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is an attached office of the Ministry of Culture.
GSLV (Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle) is primarily used by ISRO for launching geostationary satellites.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 aims to achieve universal foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) by 2025.
India is the world's third-largest producer of renewable energy.
The 'India Stack' refers to a set of open APIs and digital public goods that aim to unlock the economic primitives of identity, data, and payments.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Stupa in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has made a significant discovery, unearthing a well-preserved ancient Buddhist stupa in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• The stupa, believed to date back to the 7th-8th century CE, provides valuable insights into the spread of Buddhism in eastern India during that period.
• Excavations have also revealed intricate sculptures, pottery, and inscriptions, shedding light on the architectural and artistic styles of the time.
• This discovery adds to Odisha's rich Buddhist heritage, which includes prominent sites like Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, and Lalitgiri (the 'Diamond Triangle').
• The site is expected to become a new focal point for historical research and cultural tourism, highlighting India's diverse archaeological wealth.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
History of India and Indian National Movement
prelimsPoints: Discovery: Ancient Buddhist stupa
Location: Jajpur district, Odisha
Period: 7th-8th century CE
Discovering agency: Archaeological Survey of India (ASI)
Related sites in Odisha: Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, Lalitgiri
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in understanding India's ancient history and cultural evolution.
Examine the influence of Buddhism on the art, architecture, and social structure of early medieval India, with special reference to Eastern India.
Evaluate the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in preserving and promoting India's cultural heritage.
How can such discoveries boost cultural tourism and local economies?
GS1.imageDescription: Excavated ruins of an ancient Buddhist stupa with intricate carvings.
GS1.trivia: The 'Diamond Triangle' of Odisha (Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, Lalitgiri) is known for its extensive Buddhist monastic complexes.

GS2.headline: Veteran Politician Vijay Kumar Malhotra Passes Away at 93
GS2.explainer:
• Veteran Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) leader and former Member of Parliament, Vijay Kumar Malhotra, passed away at the age of 93.
• Malhotra had a long and distinguished political career, serving as a Member of Parliament from South Delhi constituency for multiple terms (1989–2008).
• He also served as a Member of the Delhi Legislative Assembly (2008–2013) and held various significant positions within the BJP.
• Known for his oratorical skills and deep understanding of parliamentary procedures, he was a prominent figure in Delhi and national politics.
• His demise marks the end of an era for a politician who witnessed and shaped significant political developments in post-independence India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution - historical underpinnings
Parliament and State Legislatures - structure, functioning
Prominent personalities
prelimsPoints: Name: Vijay Kumar Malhotra
Age: 93
Political party: BJP
Key roles: MP (South Delhi), MLA (Delhi)
Period of service as MP and MLA
GS2.imageDescription: File photo of Vijay Kumar Malhotra addressing a public gathering.
GS2.trivia: Vijay Kumar Malhotra was also a noted academician, having served as a professor before entering full-time politics.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Electoral Bond Scheme with Enhanced Transparency Directives
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, has largely upheld the constitutional validity of the Electoral Bond Scheme, while introducing new directives for greater transparency.
• The court mandated that all political parties must disclose the details of electoral bonds received, including donor information, to the Election Commission of India (ECI) within a specified timeframe.
• This ruling aims to balance the need for anonymous political funding, cited by the government, with the public's right to information regarding financial contributions to political parties.
• Critics argue that the scheme, even with new directives, still lacks complete transparency as donor identities are not publicly revealed by the ECI.
• The judgment will likely reshape the landscape of political funding in India, prompting debates on democratic accountability and funding reforms.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution - significant provisions
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Separation of powers
Electoral reforms
Transparency and accountability
prelimsPoints: Electoral Bond Scheme: introduced in 2018
Purpose: to cleanse political funding
Key features: bearer instrument, anonymous to public, purchased from SBI
Supreme Court's stance: upheld with transparency directives
Role of ECI in collecting donor details
mainsPoints: Analyze the Supreme Court's verdict on electoral bonds in light of democratic principles and the right to information.
Discuss the pros and cons of electoral bonds as a mechanism for political funding in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the new transparency directives in curbing illicit funding and promoting accountability.
Suggest further electoral reforms needed to ensure fair and transparent elections.
GS2.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building facade.
GS2.trivia: The State Bank of India (SBI) is the only authorized bank to issue and encash electoral bonds.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Strengthens Indo-Pacific Cooperation and Economic Ties
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming both nations' commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions focused on enhancing cooperation in defense, security, economic partnership, critical and emerging technologies, and regional connectivity projects.
• Both sides emphasized the importance of upholding international law and resolving disputes peacefully, particularly in the context of rising geopolitical tensions.
• Key outcomes included new initiatives for joint defense exercises, collaboration in clean energy, and boosting supply chain resilience.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of strategic interests between India and Japan, vital for regional stability and prosperity.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Format: Annual Strategic Dialogue
Key areas of cooperation: Defense, Economy, Tech, Connectivity
Shared vision: Free, Open, Inclusive Indo-Pacific
Other related groupings: QUAD (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue)
mainsPoints: Assess the significance of the India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the various pillars of cooperation between India and Japan, highlighting their contribution to regional security and economic development.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in deepening India-Japan defense and economic ties amidst evolving global dynamics.
How does India's 'Act East Policy' align with its strategic partnership with Japan?
GS2.imageDescription: Ministers from India and Japan shaking hands during the strategic dialogue.
GS2.trivia: Japan is one of the largest investors in India's infrastructure sector, notably the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail project.

GS2.headline: One Nation, One Ration Card Scheme Enters Phase 3, Covering All Migrant Workers
GS2.explainer:
• The 'One Nation, One Ration Card' (ONORC) scheme has successfully entered its third phase, achieving nationwide coverage to include all eligible migrant workers.
• This phase focuses on enhancing technology integration and last-mile delivery to ensure seamless access to subsidized food grains for interstate migrant beneficiaries.
• The scheme, implemented under the National Food Security Act (NFSA), allows beneficiaries to lift their entitled food grains from any Fair Price Shop (FPS) across the country.
• ONORC is a significant step towards ensuring food security and portability of benefits for India's large migrant population, reducing dependency on local ration cards.
• The government plans to further integrate other welfare schemes with ONORC to create a comprehensive safety net for vulnerable sections.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
prelimsPoints: Scheme: One Nation, One Ration Card (ONORC)
Objective: Portability of food security benefits for migrants
Legal basis: National Food Security Act (NFSA) 2013
Mechanism: Aadhaar seeding, ePOS devices at FPS
Current status: Nationwide coverage in Phase 3
mainsPoints: Evaluate the success of the 'One Nation, One Ration Card' scheme in addressing the food security challenges faced by migrant workers in India.
Discuss the technological and logistical hurdles in implementing such a large-scale portability scheme and how they have been overcome.
Analyze the socio-economic impact of ONORC on vulnerable sections of society, particularly during crises like pandemics.
Suggest further reforms needed in the Public Distribution System (PDS) to ensure more efficient and equitable delivery of food grains.
GS2.imageDescription: A person receiving ration at a Fair Price Shop using a digital biometric system.
GS2.trivia: The ONORC scheme was launched in August 2019, initially in four states, and gradually expanded.

GS2.headline: India Hosts BIMSTEC Foreign Ministers' Meeting, Focus on Regional Connectivity and Trade
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted the annual Foreign Ministers' Meeting of the Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation (BIMSTEC) in New Delhi.
• The meeting focused on accelerating regional integration, enhancing connectivity across various modes, and boosting intra-BIMSTEC trade and investment.
• Discussions included progress on the BIMSTEC Master Plan for Transport Connectivity and strategies to tackle climate change, disaster management, and health security in the region.
• India reiterated its commitment to strengthening BIMSTEC as a vital platform for regional cooperation, especially in the context of its 'Neighbourhood First' and 'Act East' policies.
• The ministers emphasized shared challenges and opportunities, aiming to build a more prosperous and resilient Bay of Bengal region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: BIMSTEC: Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation
Member countries: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, Thailand (7 countries)
Host country: India (2025 meeting)
Key focus areas: Connectivity, Trade, Disaster Management, Climate Change
Significance for India: Bridges South Asia and Southeast Asia
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of BIMSTEC for India's foreign policy, particularly in the context of its 'Neighbourhood First' and 'Act East' policies.
Discuss the potential of BIMSTEC to foster regional connectivity, trade, and economic growth in the Bay of Bengal region.
Examine the challenges hindering deeper cooperation within BIMSTEC and suggest measures to overcome them.
Compare and contrast BIMSTEC with SAARC as a regional grouping for India.
GS2.imageDescription: A logo representing the BIMSTEC organization with flags of member countries.
GS2.trivia: BIMSTEC was established in 1997 through the Bangkok Declaration.

GS2.headline: Report Highlights Progress and Challenges in National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• A comprehensive report released by the Ministry of Education details the progress made in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across states.
• The report highlights significant strides in curriculum reforms, teacher training, and integration of technology in education at various levels.
• Challenges persist in achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy, equitable access to quality education in remote areas, and adequate funding.
• The NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century, focusing on holistic development, critical thinking, and vocational skills.
• The government reaffirms its commitment to overcoming the remaining hurdles through collaborative efforts with state governments and educational institutions.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020: Replaced NEP 1986
Key pillars: Access, Equity, Quality, Affordability, Accountability
Focus areas: Early Childhood Care and Education (ECCE), FLN, vocational education, digital education
Implementation challenges: Funding, infrastructure, teacher capacity building
Goal: Transform India into a global knowledge superpower
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 in addressing the challenges of India's education system.
Discuss the key initiatives taken under NEP 2020 and their impact on various levels of education.
Examine the major hurdles in the effective implementation of NEP 2020 and suggest strategies to overcome them.
How can public-private partnerships contribute to achieving the goals set by the National Education Policy?
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting, illustrating new educational methods.
GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 recommends increasing public investment in education to 6% of GDP.

GS2.headline: Committee Recommends Sweeping Reforms in India's Prison Management System
GS2.explainer:
• A high-level committee constituted by the Ministry of Home Affairs has submitted its report recommending comprehensive reforms in India's prison management system.
• The recommendations address issues such as overcrowding, undertrial prisoner rights, staff training, use of technology, and rehabilitation programs.
• The report emphasizes the need for a human rights-based approach to incarceration and suggests amendments to the Prisons Act, 1894, to align with modern correctional philosophies.
• This initiative aims to transform prisons from mere detention centers into correctional and rehabilitative institutions, in line with global best practices.
• Implementation of these reforms is expected to significantly improve the conditions of prisoners and the overall efficiency of the criminal justice system.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
prelimsPoints: Committee: High-level committee by MHA
Focus: Prison management reforms
Key issues: Overcrowding, undertrial rights, staff training, technology, rehabilitation
Legislation to be updated: Prisons Act, 1894
Goal: Correctional and rehabilitative institutions
mainsPoints: Discuss the major challenges faced by India's prison system and how they impact human rights and the criminal justice system.
Analyze the recommendations of the high-level committee on prison reforms. How can these be effectively implemented?
Examine the concept of 'correctional administration' and its relevance in modern penology. What steps are needed to transition Indian prisons towards this model?
What is the role of technology and community participation in improving prison management and rehabilitation outcomes?
GS2.imageDescription: A general view of a prison facility from above, highlighting the need for reform.
GS2.trivia: India has one of the highest proportions of undertrial prisoners globally, with over two-thirds of inmates awaiting trial.

GS2.headline: UN General Assembly Debates Climate Financing Ahead of COP30
GS2.explainer:
• The United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) held high-level discussions on climate financing, emphasizing the urgent need for developed nations to fulfill their commitments.
• Member states debated mechanisms to mobilize greater financial resources for climate change adaptation and mitigation in developing countries, ahead of COP30.
• Developing nations, including India, reiterated calls for the operationalization of the Loss and Damage Fund and for developed countries to meet the $100 billion annual climate finance goal.
• The discussions highlighted the growing divergence between developed and developing nations on historical responsibility and current financial obligations.
• The outcome of these debates will be crucial in shaping the agenda and negotiations for the upcoming 30th Conference of the Parties (COP30).
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Forum: UN General Assembly (UNGA)
Topic: Climate Financing
Upcoming conference: COP30
Key demands from developing nations: $100 billion goal, Loss and Damage Fund operationalization
Principle: Common but Differentiated Responsibilities (CBDR)
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges and inequities in global climate financing mechanisms, particularly from the perspective of developing countries like India.
Discuss the significance of the Loss and Damage Fund and the $100 billion climate finance goal in achieving global climate justice.
Evaluate India's stance on climate change negotiations and its efforts towards both mitigation and adaptation.
How do geopolitical dynamics influence international cooperation on climate change and resource mobilization?
GS2.imageDescription: The United Nations General Assembly hall during a session on climate change.
GS2.trivia: The $100 billion annual climate finance goal was first pledged by developed countries at COP15 in Copenhagen in 2009.

GS3.headline: Under-Construction Power Plant Collapse in Tamil Nadu Claims Nine Lives
GS3.explainer:
• An arch collapse at an under-construction thermal power plant in Tamil Nadu resulted in the death of at least nine workers and left one critically injured.
• The incident occurred during the structural work of a boiler unit, raising concerns about construction safety protocols and regulatory oversight.
• Preliminary investigations point towards potential lapses in structural design or material quality, alongside inadequate safety measures for workers.
• This tragic event highlights the critical need for stricter enforcement of industrial safety standards and regular inspections in high-risk infrastructure projects.
• The state government has announced an ex-gratia payment for the victims' families and initiated a high-level inquiry into the causes of the collapse.
gsSubTopics: Disaster Management
Infrastructure
Industrial Policy
Role of regulatory bodies
prelimsPoints: Location of the incident (Tamil Nadu)
Type of plant (under-construction thermal power plant)
Number of casualties
Regulatory bodies involved in industrial safety (e.g., Directorate General of Factory Advice Service & Labour Institutes - DGFASLI)
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the challenges in ensuring industrial safety in India's rapidly expanding infrastructure sector.
Discuss the role of government, private contractors, and labor unions in preventing such accidents.
Examine the effectiveness of existing disaster management protocols and compensation mechanisms for industrial accidents.
Suggest measures for strengthening regulatory frameworks and enforcement to prevent future mishaps.
GS3.imageDescription: Rescue operations underway at the collapsed power plant site in Tamil Nadu.
GS3.trivia: India is the third-largest producer and second-largest consumer of electricity globally, with thermal power accounting for a significant share of its generation capacity.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Persistent Inflationary Pressures
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the fifth consecutive meeting.
• The decision comes as the central bank continues to prioritize inflation control, despite signs of moderating economic growth.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted global uncertainties and volatile food prices as key risks to the inflation outlook.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Experts anticipate the RBI to maintain a cautious stance, with rate cuts unlikely until inflation shows a sustained downward trend.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Current Repo Rate: 6.5% (unchanged)
Number of consecutive meetings unchanged: 5th
Primary objective of RBI's monetary policy: Price stability (inflation control)
Tools used by RBI: Repo rate, reverse repo rate, CRR, SLR, OMO
Composition of MPC: 6 members (3 RBI, 3 GoI nominees)
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflation in India.
Examine the implications of a prolonged high-interest rate regime on various sectors of the Indian economy.
Evaluate the role of the Monetary Policy Committee in maintaining financial stability and fostering sustainable growth.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The MPC was constituted in India in 2016 to bring transparency and accountability in deciding monetary policy.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Weather Satellite INSAT-3DRS
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched the advanced geostationary weather satellite, INSAT-3DRS, from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
• Carried by the GSLV-F15 rocket, the satellite is designed to provide enhanced meteorological observations and disaster warning services for India and the Indian Ocean region.
• INSAT-3DRS features advanced imaging systems and atmospheric sounders, significantly improving weather forecasting capabilities and cyclone tracking.
• This mission reinforces India's self-reliance in space technology and its commitment to contributing to global weather and climate monitoring efforts.
• The data from INSAT-3DRS will be crucial for agriculture, disaster management, and various climate research applications.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
Disaster Management
prelimsPoints: Satellite name: INSAT-3DRS
Launch vehicle: GSLV-F15
Purpose: Advanced meteorological observations, disaster warning
Orbit type: Geostationary
Launch site: Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's space program in contributing to national development and disaster management.
Evaluate the role of advanced weather satellites like INSAT-3DRS in improving climate resilience and agricultural productivity.
Examine how ISRO's indigenous capabilities in space technology contribute to India's strategic autonomy.
What are the future prospects for India's space program, particularly in Earth observation and planetary exploration?
GS3.imageDescription: A GSLV rocket launching into space from a launchpad.
GS3.trivia: India's first dedicated meteorological satellite, Kalpana-1 (formerly MetSat-1), was launched in 2002.

GS3.headline: NGT Imposes Hefty Fines on Industries for Environmental Violations in Ganga Basin
GS3.explainer:
• The National Green Tribunal (NGT) has imposed significant penalties on several industries located along the Ganga basin for continuous discharge of untreated effluents.
• The NGT's ruling emphasized the 'Polluter Pays' principle and directed immediate measures for compliance with environmental norms and installation of effluent treatment plants.
• This action comes amidst ongoing efforts to rejuvenate the Ganga River and curb industrial pollution, a major challenge for the Namami Gange programme.
• The tribunal also highlighted the failure of local authorities in effective monitoring and enforcement of environmental regulations.
• The judgment underscores the NGT's proactive role in environmental protection and holding polluters accountable for ecological damage.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: National Green Tribunal (NGT): established in 2010, specialized body for environmental justice
Principle applied: Polluter Pays Principle
Affected region: Ganga basin
Related government program: Namami Gange Programme
NGT's powers: can impose fines, issue directions
mainsPoints: Evaluate the effectiveness of the National Green Tribunal (NGT) in upholding environmental justice and promoting sustainable development in India.
Discuss the major challenges in controlling industrial pollution, particularly in river basins like the Ganga, and suggest remedial measures.
Analyze the 'Polluter Pays' principle and its application in Indian environmental law. What are its limitations?
Critically examine the progress and challenges of the Namami Gange programme.
GS3.imageDescription: A polluted stretch of a river with industrial discharge in the background.
GS3.trivia: The NGT has been instrumental in several landmark judgments, including banning 10-year-old diesel and 15-year-old petrol vehicles in Delhi-NCR.

GS3.headline: India's Q2 GDP Growth Projected at 7.5%, Driven by Services and Investment
GS3.explainer:
• Leading economic agencies and the government's finance ministry have projected India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth for Q2 FY2025 (July-September) at 7.5%.
• This robust growth is primarily attributed to strong performance in the services sector, resilient domestic demand, and a noticeable uptick in private capital expenditure.
• Manufacturing and construction sectors also showed positive momentum, signaling a broad-based recovery and expansion across key economic drivers.
• While global headwinds persist, India's domestic consumption and public investment continue to act as significant buffers, sustaining economic buoyancy.
• The projection reinforces India's position as one of the fastest-growing major economies globally, albeit with ongoing concerns about inflation and fiscal consolidation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Inclusive Growth
prelimsPoints: Q2 FY2025 period: July-September
Projected GDP growth rate: 7.5%
Key drivers: Services, private investment, domestic demand
Economic challenges: Inflation, global headwinds
India's global ranking: Fastest-growing major economy
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's sustained economic growth despite global uncertainties.
Discuss the role of different sectors (agriculture, industry, services) in India's GDP growth and their interlinkages.
Examine the challenges that India needs to address to achieve inclusive and sustainable economic growth.
Evaluate the government's fiscal and monetary policies in fostering economic stability and growth.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphical representation of India's economic growth trend.
GS3.trivia: India's economy is projected to become the third-largest in the world by 2027.

GS3.headline: BRO Completes Strategic Shinku La Tunnel, Enhancing All-Weather Connectivity to Ladakh
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) has announced the completion of the strategic Shinku La Tunnel, providing all-weather connectivity to the Zanskar Valley in Ladakh.
• This high-altitude tunnel, located at over 16,000 feet, is a critical infrastructure project for national security and regional development.
• It significantly reduces travel time and improves accessibility to remote border areas, crucial for both military logistics and local communities.
• The tunnel will bypass the challenging Shinku La pass, which remains snow-bound for much of the year, ensuring year-round access.
• This engineering marvel is part of India's broader strategy to enhance border infrastructure and integrate remote regions into the national mainstream.
gsSubTopics: Border areas and their management
Security challenges and their management in border areas
Infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Tunnel name: Shinku La Tunnel
Location: Zanskar Valley, Ladakh (connecting Himachal Pradesh to Ladakh)
Executing agency: Border Roads Organisation (BRO)
Significance: All-weather connectivity, strategic importance
Altitude: Over 16,000 feet
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of developing all-weather connectivity and border infrastructure in regions like Ladakh for India's national security.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) in constructing infrastructure in high-altitude and difficult terrains.
Evaluate the impact of such infrastructure projects on the socio-economic development of border communities.
What role does infrastructure play in integrating remote regions into the national mainstream and ensuring territorial integrity?
GS3.imageDescription: An entrance to a newly constructed tunnel in a mountainous region.
GS3.trivia: The BRO is responsible for constructing and maintaining road networks in India's border areas and friendly neighboring countries.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Policy Framework for Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Power has released a comprehensive new policy framework aimed at accelerating the deployment of Electric Vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure across India.
• The policy focuses on streamlining licensing and regulatory processes, offering incentives for private investment, and standardizing charging technologies.
• Key provisions include mandatory charging stations in new commercial and residential buildings, land availability for public charging, and time-bound installation targets.
• This move is critical for boosting EV adoption, reducing carbon emissions, and achieving India's ambitious climate change goals.
• The framework seeks to create a robust and accessible charging ecosystem, addressing one of the primary barriers to EV proliferation.
